Stats — The Free Open-Source macOS Menu Bar System Monitor I Use Every Day (CPU, GPU, Temperature, Network)

A walkthrough of Stats — the open-source macOS menu bar system monitor I run on every Mac I own. Covers what it shows (CPU, GPU, RAM, disk, network, battery, sensor temperatures, Bluetooth, multi-timezone clock, plus a Remote module), how to install it with `brew install --cask stats`, a quick tour through each module, the desktop widget trick, and a couple of macOS 26 widget quirks worth knowing about.
How Should You Install Software on a Mac? Homebrew vs Official Download vs curl

If you've just started using a Mac for development, you've probably noticed there are several different ways to install the same tool — download a `.dmg` from the official site, install with Homebrew, run a `curl` install script, and tutorials sometimes recommend different approaches for the same tool. This post sorts through the common Mac install methods, pairs them with real examples (Git, Node.js, Python, VS Code), so you know which one to pick when.
Raycast Extensions I Actually Use — Level Up Your Raycast

A friend asked why my project switching and dev workflow feels so snappy on Raycast — turns out it's mostly the extensions. This post is a rundown of the Raycast extensions I actually keep installed: TinyPNG, Kill Process, Visual Studio Code, Google Translate, Speedtest, Format JSON, Base64, and JWT Decoder — with my aliases for each.
Replace Mac's Built-In Spotlight With Raycast

Raycast is a Mac app that can fully replace Spotlight — and it's way more capable. This post walks through installing Raycast, swapping Spotlight's Cmd+Space hotkey over to Raycast, the basic features (open apps, calculator), setting per-app aliases, using Raycast as a Google search bar, the clipboard history feature, and installing extensions like Google Translate from the Raycast Store.
Homebrew zsh: command not found: brew — How I Fixed It

One day brew install just stopped working — zsh told me it couldn't find "brew install", but plain `brew` did show output. This post walks through what was wrong (PATH-related setup against the wrong shell rc file, plus a missing wget dependency), and the steps that actually got brew install back up and running.
BetterDiscord: How to Customize and Power Up Your Discord

Discord keeps blowing up — even companies are dropping Slack for it. But sometimes the default UI just isn't enough, and you want a more comfortable, customised setup. This post walks through using BetterDiscord to customise and enhance your Discord on macOS.
Fixing Mac XAMPP Apache Won't Start
XAMPP is a great one-stop PHP local dev tool, but recently MySQL was starting fine while Apache Web Server just hung. This post is my notes on what was going wrong.
Forced to Eat Mango (2): MongoDB Basics in the Terminal
The Frontend Engineer Forced to Eat Mango

Before touching any fancy GUI, it's worth getting comfortable with MongoDB straight from the terminal. This post walks through the basic commands — listing databases, switching between them, showing collections — and the slightly weird thing where switching to a database that doesn't exist just works. Once you know these basics, the GUI tools will feel a lot nicer.
The Frontend Dev Forced to Eat Mango (1): Meeting MongoDB and Installing It
The Frontend Dev Forced to Eat Mango

Time for another learning log, and this time it's all about MongoDB. Since "Mongo" sounds a lot like "mango" in Chinese, I named this series "The Fullstack Dev Forced to Eat Mango." In this first post I'll cover what MongoDB actually is, how it differs from SQL, what BSON is, and how to install and run it on both Mac and Windows.
How to Clear the Terminal Screen (Mac and Windows)

Whether you're on Mac or Windows, the terminal piles up output fast and it gets noisy. Quick note on the commands and keyboard shortcuts that clear the screen — including the distinction between "actually wiping scrollback" vs. "just scrolling the cursor down so the screen looks empty".
